name: yay
description: Yet Another Yogurt — a fast AUR helper written in Go with pacman-compatible syntax and support for development packages.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git && cd yay && makepkg -si

name: paru
description: Feature-rich AUR helper written in Rust. Supports review diffs, PKGBUILD editing, and news reading. Considered the modern successor to yay.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/paru.git && cd paru && makepkg -si

name: trizen
description: Lightweight AUR helper written in Perl with interactive search and a focus on simplicity.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/trizen.git && cd trizen && makepkg -si

name: pikaur
description: AUR helper that builds all AUR packages in a single makepkg call to avoid privilege escalation loops. Written in Python.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/pikaur.git && cd pikaur && makepkg -si

name: bauerbill
description: Advanced AUR and pacman wrapper with dependency resolution and blacklisting support. Extends powerpill.

name: makepkg
description: The official Arch Linux package build tool. Reads PKGBUILDs to compile and package software. Ships with pacman.

name: aurutils
description: A collection of scripts for managing local AUR package repositories, building, and syncing. Designed for power users.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/aurutils.git && cd aurutils && makepkg -si

name: pacaur
description: Minimalist AUR helper focused on minimal interaction. Uses cower under the hood. Largely unmaintained.
install-cmd: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/pacaur.git && cd pacaur && makepkg -si

name: pamac
description: Graphical and CLI package manager from Manjaro with AUR, Flatpak, and Snap support. Includes a GTK GUI application.

name: asp
description: Arch Source Package tool — fetches PKGBUILDs from the official Arch Linux SVN repository. Useful for inspecting or modifying official packages.
